stories.headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 lands on AWS with Mythos-level performance
stories.blurb: Amazon Bedrock now offers Claude Fable 5, Anthropic's latest model that brings their top-tier Mythos capabilities to all customers. The model excels at coding, knowledge work, and vision tasks, available through both Bedrock and Claude Platform on AWS.
stories.why_it_matters: Anyone building on AWS can now access Anthropic's most capable model without waiting for enterprise access.
stories.original_headline: Claude Fable 5 from Anthropic now available on AWS

stories.headline: Google just shipped a model that writes text 4x faster than GPT-style generation
stories.blurb: DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a new approach that generates all tokens at once instead of one-by-one like ChatGPT. It's 4x faster than standard autoregressive models and matches quality on coding and reasoning tasks. The model is open-weight and runs on standard hardware.
stories.why_it_matters: Faster inference means cheaper API costs and snappier apps without buying new chips.

stories.headline: Apple is now running its AI on NVIDIA chips in Google's cloud
stories.blurb: Apple's Private Cloud Compute — the system that handles complex Siri requests and other AI tasks too big for your iPhone — is expanding from Apple's own data centers to Google Cloud, running on NVIDIA GPUs with confidential computing. The setup lets Apple process sensitive user data on third-party infrastructure while keeping it encrypted even during computation.
stories.why_it_matters: Apple just admitted it needs Google's infrastructure to scale its AI ambitions.
stories.original_headline: NVIDIA Confidential Computing to Help Expand Apple’s Private Cloud Compute

stories.headline: Cursor's AI code reviewer just got 3x faster and finds 10% more bugs
stories.blurb: Cursor upgraded Bugbot to catch more issues before you push — it's now three times faster, 22% cheaper to run, and finds 10% more bugs per review. A new /review command lets you trigger a full code review on demand instead of waiting for the automated scan.
stories.why_it_matters: Faster feedback loops mean you catch bugs before they hit CI, not after.
stories.original_headline: Bugbot is now over 3x faster, 22% cheaper, and finds 10% more bugs

stories.headline: Google's new Gemma 4 can see, hear, and code in one 12B model
stories.blurb: DeepMind released Gemma 4 12B, a single model that handles text, images, and audio without needing separate encoders for each format. It matches larger models on vision tasks while running on consumer hardware, and developers can fine-tune it for custom multimodal applications.
stories.why_it_matters: Multimodal AI that fits on a laptop means builders can ship apps that see and hear without cloud costs.
stories.original_headline: Introducing Gemma 4 12B: a unified, encoder-free multimodal model

try_this_prompt.title: The research rabbit hole escape
try_this_prompt.prompt: I've been researching a topic for longer than I planned. I'll describe what I'm trying to figure out below. Tell me: the one question my research should actually answer, three sources I should read and nothing else, and the decision I'm probably avoiding by reading more.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: When you've got 47 tabs open and still no clarity.

daily_question: If you could swap one AI model's speed for another's accuracy, which trade would you make?
